Waveform Audio

React audio UI library for waveform playback and waveform recording.

Public package
- `@waveform-audio/player`

Stable imports
- `Player`
- `Recorder`
- `PlayerRoot`
- public primitives exported by the package
- `useAudioPlayer()`
- `useAudioRecorder()`
- `useGlobalAudioManager()`

Use this API layering
1. Start with `Player` or `Recorder`
2. Move to `PlayerRoot` only when custom playback layout is required
3. Move to hooks only when the app owns workflow, orchestration, upload, or ASR session logic

Practical prompting pattern
- First say whether the task needs a player or a recorder
- Then say whether the default UI is enough or whether custom layout, multi-instance control, upload, or ASR is required
- Ask for the smallest working integration first, then customize only if needed

Supported scenarios
- default waveform audio player in React
- branded player UI with public primitives
- multiple coordinated players on one page
- waveform recorder with review playback
- file-level ASR handoff
- streaming ASR with chunk events

Recorder integration rules
- Use `onRecordingComplete()` for file-level upload or offline ASR
- Use `timeslice` + `onSessionStart` + `onChunk` + `onSessionEnd` for streaming ASR
- Use `waveformData` and `level` for recorder UI instead of building a second analyzer
- Use `blobUrl` for local preview, `file` / `blob` for persistence or upload

Never do this
- do not import from `libs/player-react/src/*`
- do not import from `dist/*`
- do not rely on repo-relative source paths
- do not recommend compatibility aliases as the default public API

Starter prompts
- `Use @waveform-audio/player to add a waveform audio player to this React app. Start with the default Player component. Read /llms.txt before writing code, and do not import internal source paths.`
- `Use @waveform-audio/player to add a waveform recorder to this React app. Start with the default Recorder component. If the task involves upload or ASR, read /llms.txt before writing code and stay on public APIs.`
